sutta: once the exalted 1 with a great company of monks while wandering for arms among the kosalis came to the brahmin village of icha nanggala belonging to them and there the exalted 1 dwelt in the icha nangala woodland ticket now the brahmin householders of icha nangala heard of this mount you they said the recluse master gotama the sakyan gone forth from the sakin clan has come to isha nangala and dwells in our wood nearby and of this same master gautama this excellent report goes abroad he is the exalted 1 arahan perfectly enlightened endowed with knowledge and practice well gone 1 world noah unsurpassed tamar of tamable men teacher teacher of devas and men buddha the exalted 1 having realized higher knowledge himself he declares it to this world with his devas and maras and brahmas to this earth with its recluses and brahmins its devas and men and he teaches dhamma lovely in the beginning lovely in the middle and lovely in the end and he sets forth the holy life utterly pure and perfect both in spirit and letter well indeed it is it to see such arahants and when the night was over they went to the wood taking with them a great deal of hard and soft food and stood outside the entrance making a great din and up now at that time the venable nagita was the exalted 1's personal attendant then the exalted 1 called to the venable nagita and said who are these nagita that make this great din and up raw like fishermen i think with a great hall of fish these men lord are the brahmin householders of each and angela who wait outside the gateway they have brought much hard and soft food for the exalted 1 and for the sangha and the buddha said i have nothing to do with homage nageeta nor has homage anything to do with me whosoever cannot obtain at will easily and without difficulty this happiness of renunciation this happiness of seclusion this happiness of calm this happiness of enlightenment which i can obtain at will easily and without difficulty let him enjoy that dung-like happiness that sluggish happiness that happiness gotten of gains favors and flattery lord let the exalted 1 accept their offerings let the well-gone 1 accept now lord is the time for the blessed 1 to accept for wheres whoever henceforth the exalted 1 shall go the brahmana householders of town and country will be just as inclined to give lord jesus when the rain deva rains big drops the water flows in with the incline even so lord wheresoever the exalted 1 shall henceforth go the brahmin householders of town and country will be inclined and why lord it is because of the virtue and wisdom of the exalted 1 and the buddha said again now gita i have nothing to do with homage no has so much anything to do with me whosoever cannot obtain at will easily and without difficulty this happiness of renunciation this happiness of seclusion this happiness of calm this happiness of enlightenment which i can obtain at will easily and without difficulty let him enjoy the dung-like happiness that sluggish happiness that happiness gotten of gains favors and flattery verily navita whosoever eats and drinks munches and crunches must answer the calls of nature such as the issue of it whosoever loves to him change and separation must come grief lamentation suffering sorrow and despair such is the issue of it whosoever is bent on applying himself to the sign of the unattractive in him discuss for the sign of the attractiveness established such as the issue of it whosoever abides seeing impermanence in the 6 fears of contact in him disgust for content is established such as the issue of it whosoever abides seeing the rise and fall in the 5-fold aggregates of attachment in him disgust for attachment is established such is the issue of it as the end of the sutta
commentary:
this is an interesting suta it shows that the buddha likes the peace and quietness and he doesn't like noisy people around him that's why in the early in the morning when all these brahmins came with a lot of offerings because they made so much noise the buddha said they are just like fishermen and refused to accept their offerings and this nageeta is attending i was trying to persuade the buddha to accept the offerings so that later and more wherever they go people also will give a lot of offerings but the buddha tried the buddha wasn't happy to accept the buddha said but nothing to do with that kind of happiness the buddha said his kind of happiness is the happiness of renunciation happiness of seclusion the happiness of calm the happiness of enlightenment and this refers to the jaanas the janas are the happiness of renunciation the happiness of seclusion happiness of enlightenment even so the buddha is more concerned with getting this happiness that surpasses the world rather than worldly happiness so you see sometimes the buddha can refuse to accept offerings it is not conducive to the holy life as in this case prefer that they allow him to practice in seclusion and give him more privacy and he's willing to accept less offerings and then the last part of it the buddha gave some interesting teaching the buddha says that whosoever loves her to him or her change and separation must come grief lamentation suffering sorrow and despair most of us we like to love somebody but when you love somebody you have to pay for it because change and separation must come what is this change this change refers to that you love somebody but after some time somebody might not love you or fall out of love with you and then you'll get a heartbreak or separation comes for example you love your child maybe your child passes away and you get a lot of grief so if you have love grief lamentation suffering sorrow and despair will come and then if you practice under the sign the asuba the sign of the unattractive to see the lonesomeness of the body then you will have discuss for the sign of the attractive that means the sign of beauty loses the appeal to you if you constantly see the unattractiveness of the body the load simpleness of the body and then if you constantly try to see impermanence in the 6 spheres of contact that means in the sixth sense doors seeing hearing smelling taste touch and thinking if you constantly see impermanence on constant change under the sixth sense doors then disgust for contact is established and we have discussed for contact now you go into the mind you go into the inner self you try to cut off the outer world that is the going back into the inner cell that is the way to go back to to peace to nibana because the state of nibana is separate from the world the world is the buddha and the first noble truth the buddha said all existence is suffering that's why we have to run away from existence and then if we constantly see the arising and uh decay of the 5 aggregates of attachment that means the 5 aggregates uh pancha kanda this 1 is body feeling perception volition and consciousness these 5 things is very important to see how they change and then you if you can see how they constantly change and then slowly you let go of the associating the self and we with these 5 things
